title: [riscv64] `Exec format error` emulating Debian
body:
### Issue Description

I don't seem to be able to emulate Debian riscv64.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -it --arch riscv64 debian


### Describe the results you received

```console
% podman run -it --arch riscv64 debian
{"msg":"exec container process `/usr/bin/bash`: Exec format error","level":"error","time":"2025-10-25T10:07:46.123281Z"}
```

### Describe the results you expected

A shell prompt is displayed.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

M2 Pro MacBook Pro

### Additional information

The similar command, `docker run -it --platform linux/riscv64 debian`, works.

comments.body:
A similar issue seems to happen with `container`:

```console
% container run --arch riscv64 -it debian    
Error: internalError: "Failed to exec [bash] Error Domain=NSPOSIXErrorDomain Code=8 "Exec format error""
```

> Did you install the `qemu-riscv64-static` program?

I did not. How do I install it?

Some [Fedora CoreOS](https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/) command, after `podman machine ssh`?

```yaml
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: coreos
    version: "42"
```
Thx, `sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static-riscv` and restarting the machine fixed it.

I hit a similar issue with ppc64le and s390x.  The following worked for me as it installed all the emulators:

```
podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static
```
